hypothesis: Systematic fine-tuning of an LLM on a curated dataset of CALPHAD-computed thermodynamic trajectories and phase transformation sequences across multiple binary and ternary oxide systems will produce a domain-specialized model whose synthesis route success rates significantly exceed those of general-purpose GPT-4o, specifically because the fine-tuned model will develop accurate implicit representations of the nonlinear mapping between composition-temperature control actions and resulting phase fractions, thereby eliminating the failure mode observed in Challenge 2 where the LLM correctly identified target phases but could not achieve precise stoichiometric fractions.
